data.title: Distributed Systems Design
data.description: Google's architecture utilizes a distributed system design to manage its vast network of data centers worldwide.

data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Google uses a microservices approach to scale and manage its services efficiently.

data.title: Global CDN
data.description: Content delivery network to ensure fast load times and low latency by caching content globally.

data.title: High Availability Infrastructure
data.description: Ensures that Google services are resilient and available 24/7 with minimal downtime.
data.technical_spec: Redundancy, failover mechanisms, geo-diverse data centers

data.title: Scalable Storage Solutions
data.description: Uses proprietary databases like Bigtable and Spanner for scalable and distributed storage.
data.technical_spec: NoSQL databases, SQL layer on key-value stores, consistency models
